root_cause.headline: Pacific routes share a salt-air corrosion pattern the Atlantic fleet's coastal stops don't see — the December gasket vendor switch correlates 91% with elevated removals
root_cause.detail: Of 38 Pacific-fleet servo removals in the 60-day window, 35 share a single signature: the gasket vendor changed in December from a fluorocarbon compound to a nitrile compound (vendor code GKT-N41) that degrades 3.2x faster in sustained salt-air exposure above 400 cycles/month. Atlantic routes average 180 cycles/month through similar coastal altitudes; Pacific routes average 490. The fix is a gasket revert to the fluorocarbon compound — not a servo recall.
root_cause.affected_cohort: Pacific-corridor tails with gasket vendor GKT-N41 post December change and cycles greater than 400/month

without_wizard.label: Without dbt-wizard
without_wizard.summary: Question enters the analytics backlog. Airworthiness review meets without the answer.
narrative: CRO files the request. It enters the analytics backlog.
Data engineer scopes the work — days after the review window.
Engineer writes SQL from scratch across four gold marts by hand.
Tests, YAML, and contract get bolted on at PR time.
Reliability team cannot isolate root cause before the airworthiness review.
Fleet grounding exposure and AOG costs continue to accrue.

with_wizard.label: With dbt-wizard
with_wizard.summary: Four sub-agents surface the upstreams, author the SQL, write the tests, and materialize the asset in 90 seconds.
narrative: Explorer runs status and search. Returns the four gold upstream candidates.
Summary runs describe and lineage. Confirms join path and names the new grain.
Worker runs dbt_show on a 60-day slice. Validates the proposed grain.
Worker authors fct_removal_by_part_route_corridor_60d.sql with CTEs and final SELECT.
Verification writes the YAML — description, schema contract, 8 tests, ownership.
Worker materializes the table to Iceberg. Verification confirms downstream lineage.
CRO presents gasket-revert finding to Airworthiness Review 12 hours ahead of schedule.

hero.label: Root cause identified 12 hours before the Airworthiness Review — gasket revert, not a servo recall
hero.note: From CRO question to materialized Iceberg gold table — versioned, tested, documented, lineage-tracked. The reliability team now knows Pacific salt-air exposure combined with the December gasket vendor switch is the driver. The fix is a gasket revert to the fluorocarbon compound. $11M in AOG and fleet-grounding exposure is resolved without a full servo recall.
